title: Platyelasma densum
taxonomy: Platyelasma densum is classified as a species of lichen in the family Graphidaceae. The family Graphidaceae belongs to the order Ostropales. The order Ostropales is part of the class Lecanoromycetes. The class Lecanoromycetes is within the phylum Ascomycota. This classification helps understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Platyelasma densum.

intro: Platyelasma densum, also known as Indian gentian or gentianella densa, is a plant species that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can aid in the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as provide protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Platyelasma densum include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
